description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a bulging midsection, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by three prominent black metal hoops—one at the top, one at the bottom, and one around the widest part. The wood has a natural, slightly glossy finish with visible grain patterns and some subtle color variation, suggesting it is made from oak or a similar hardwood. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and appear to be made from the same wood as the staves, tightly fitted within the metal bands. There are no visible handles, spouts, or openings, and the barrel is fully enclosed. The metal hoops provide structural reinforcement and a visual contrast to the warm wood tones. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage areas, or as decorative elements in parks and public spaces.
description: A wooden barrel with black metal hoops, bulging sides, and a flat, closed top and bottom. The wood is light brown with visible grain, and the metal bands reinforce the structure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full bulging profile, three black metal hoops, and the flat wooden top.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view, highlighting the curvature of the staves and the seamless fit of the top within the hoops.
description_view_2: Side view with a clear look at the midsection's widest point and the even spacing of the metal bands.
description_view_3: Another angled view, similar to view 1, emphasizing the wood grain and the continuous banding.
